title: Benchmark Storage Media Tower
summary: A tall, narrow plywood storage tower with fixed shelves, a locking Euro-hinge door, and an acrylic-reinforced door panel, sized to flank a media center or stand alone.
description:
Ripping the whole plywood sheet into 15-3/4"-wide strips up front is what makes the rest of this build fast — sides, shelves, and top all come off the same rip, so there's no repeated setup and every shelf lands the same depth without extra measuring. Marking and drilling every pocket hole before any assembly begins matters more here than in a simple box: once the sides, legs, and trim are joined, several of those holes become impossible to reach.

The door gets a layer of 1/4" acrylic glass screwed to the back of its frame — not decorative, structural. A frame this size built from 1x3 alone would rack and flex on its hinges; the acrylic panel stiffens it the way a true door panel would, at a fraction of the cost and weight. Leaving an 1/8" gap on every side of the door is what keeps it swinging free once that added stiffness is in place.

tools.note: Predrills the acrylic glass panel before mounting.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 15-3/4"-wide strips for the sides, shelves, and top.

steps.title: Rip the plywood and mark all pocket holes
steps.body: Rip the plywood sheet into 15-3/4"-wide strips for the sides, shelves, and top. Before any assembly, go through the whole plan and mark and drill every pocket hole — several become unreachable once the carcass is joined. On each side, on the outside edges with no pocket holes, attach the side trim to the top and bottom with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue, flush to the outside edges. You need two identical sides.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: With top edges flush, attach the legs to both sides using 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the front and back trim
steps.body: Measure and mark the placement of the front and back trim boards, then attach with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the fixed shelves
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on all sides of the fixed shelves. Attach with the side trim, flush at the tops, using 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Build the top with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, then screw it on from the underside with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Cut the plywood back to fit and attach with glue and 1-1/4" finish nails, making sure to catch every fixed shelf. Cut any cord-pass holes now, before the back is closed in.

steps.title: Build the door frame
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes on each end of the shorter door pieces and build the door frame with glue and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Reinforce the door with acrylic glass
steps.body: Predrill holes in the acrylic glass and attach it to the back side of the door frame — this significantly stiffens the door. Mark the hinge locations first and notch the acrylic (or cut it slightly short on the hinge side) so the hinges have a clean, flat mounting spot.

steps.title: Hang the door
steps.body: Mount the Euro-style concealed hinges, which need generous clearance — test-fit before committing. Leave a 1/8" gap on every side of the door so it swings freely, add the knob, sand every surface, and finish.

images.alt: Tall plywood storage media tower with an acrylic-paneled door
